core_model.structure_summary: An ensemble of monotonically growing random counting trajectories is summarized by the mean first time at which trajectories reach a given level, and the derivative of this mean first-passage time with respect to the level detects a critical threshold where the growth regime qualitatively changes.
core_model.canonical_form: \tau(x) = \frac{1}{M} \sum_{i=1}^{M} t_i(x), \qquad t_i(x) = \inf\{t : x_i(t) \ge x\}, \qquad \text{threshold from behavior of } \partial \tau(x)/\partial x

variables.meaning: cumulative count of each realization, non-decreasing in time
continuous or discrete independent variable along each trajectory
target level at which first passage is recorded
number of realizations in the ensemble average
mean first-passage time as a function of the target level, whose slope locates the critical threshold

notes: Very few equations were extracted; the paper is a methodology/data-analysis paper rather than one proposing a closed-form dynamical model. The core object is the mean first-passage time statistic of an ensemble of growing counting processes, with the regime-change threshold identified from the level-derivative of that statistic. The probability_flow and jump_process tags are inferred from the first-passage framework rather than from explicit displayed equations.
